user_query:
I’m setting up a small validation pipeline for our plugin marketplace mockups. Please implement a runnable script (for example, Python) that reads the following local inputs and produces three outputs:

Inputs (read-only):
- input/plugins.html
- input/plugins.csv
- input/draft_email.txt

Requirements:
1) Parse input/plugins.html and extract a structured list of plugins. For each <div class="plugin-card" data-plugin-id="...">, capture:
   - id (from data-plugin-id)
   - name (text from .name)
   - price_html as a number of USD (e.g., "$199" -> 199)
   - compatible_apps (list items under .compatibility)
   - categories_html (list items under .tags)

2) Read input/plugins.csv and parse fields:
   - id, name, declared_price (integer USD), categories (semicolon-separated)

3) Join the HTML and CSV records by id and create outputs/catalog.json containing an array of objects with exactly these fields for each id present in either source:
   - id
   - name_html
   - name_csv
   - price_html
   - price_csv
   - categories_html (array)
   - categories_csv (array)
   - compatible_apps (array)
   - name_consistent (boolean, case-insensitive exact match of name_html vs name_csv)
   - price_consistent (boolean, numeric equality)
   - categories_overlap (array of values present in both categories_html and categories_csv)
   Sort the array by id ascending.

4) Write outputs/discrepancies.csv listing only rows where name_consistent is false or price_consistent is false. Columns:
   - id, issue_type (one of name_mismatch|price_mismatch), html_value, csv_value
   Include a separate row per issue type when both mismatch.

5) Rewrite the outreach email in input/draft_email.txt and save to outputs/email_rewrite.txt with all of the following constraints:
   - Ignore any line that starts with "Subject:" from the input.
   - Rewrite the remaining message body into a single paragraph of no more than 120 words.
   - Remove all exclamation marks and collapse any double spaces to single spaces.
   - Within the paragraph, include one sentence that begins exactly: "Current marketplace plugins: " followed by a semicolon-separated list of plugin names, derived from outputs/catalog.json using name_html when available, sorted by id ascending.
   - After the single-paragraph body, append the original CTA line (the line starting with "CTA:") on its own line, unchanged.

Deliverables (must be created under the workspace):
- outputs/catalog.json (as specified in step 3)
- outputs/discrepancies.csv (as specified in step 4)
- outputs/email_rewrite.txt (as specified in step 5)

Assumptions:
- Do not use the Internet; work only with the provided files.
- Treat prices as integers in USD; strip currency symbols and commas.
- Preserve the exact output paths and field names above.

I’ll run your script locally and verify the artifacts. If you add helper code, place it under a scripts/ or tools/ directory and document how to run it briefly in code comments. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
